items.title: Les Misérables
items.description: The acclaimed Broadway production of Les Misérables returns to Seattle. This epic musical tells the story of Jean Valjean and his journey through 19th-century France.

items.title: Westerman, Otto Benson
items.description: Indie folk artist Westerman performs with Otto Benson at this intimate Ballard venue.

items.title: Nettspend
items.description: Rising hip-hop artist Nettspend brings his energetic live show to Seattle.

items.title: True Loves with Special Guest Nigel Hall (LETTUCE)
items.description: Seattle's own True Loves perform their soulful funk sound with special guest Nigel Hall from the band LETTUCE.

items.title: Fcukers
items.description: Electronic music act Fcukers performs at this Capitol Hill favorite venue.

items.title: Duo Comedy Showcase: The Duo Olympics
items.description: Seattle's one and only open mic dedicated to two-person improv teams. Two-person teams take the stage to create hilarious, fast-paced, totally unscripted comedy.

items.title: 700 Level Class Showcase
items.description: Improv showcase featuring students from Unexpected Productions' advanced classes performing their newly honed skills.

items.title: Improv Greenhouse
items.description: An improv show where new formats and experimental comedy are tested in front of a live audience.

items.title: 5th Annual John Prine Tribute Night
items.description: Seattle artists play their favorite John Prine songs, with Maywood Mailmen performing 'German Afternoons' in its entirety.

items.title: Magic Across America Tour with Josh Farley
items.description: Comedy magician Josh Farley brings his Magic Across America tour to Seattle for an evening of illusions and laughs.

items.title: Westerlies Fest 2026 – 'Have You Heard?' Album Release Show
items.description: The Westerlies brass quartet celebrates their new album release with a special performance at this unique Fremont venue.

items.title: Satsang (Solo)
items.description: Montana-based roots reggae artist Satsang performs a solo acoustic set in Bellingham, just north of Seattle.

items.title: Open Rehearsal: Guest Conductor Peter Oundjian
items.description: Watch the Seattle Symphony rehearse with guest conductor Peter Oundjian. A rare behind-the-scenes look at the orchestra at work.

items.title: STG Dance for PD Spring Quarter
items.description: Dance for Parkinson's Disease - a specialized dance program for individuals with Parkinson's and their families.

items.title: Broadway Inclusion Program at Northshore
items.description: STG's Broadway Inclusion Program provides musical theater arts programming for individuals with neurodivergent needs.

items.title: The Bolshoi Brothers / Theatre of Hate
items.description: Post-punk legends Theatre of Hate perform with The Bolshoi Brothers at this historic Seattle rock venue.

items.title: Sosocamo
items.description: Up-and-coming artist Sosocamo performs at this intimate downstairs venue beneath Neumos on Capitol Hill.

items.title: Mason Marcus Turner + J. Killen + The Rolling Thunder
items.description: Local folk and Americana artists perform at this historic Ballard pub known for live music.

items.title: Small Yards, The Hugs, Media House
items.description: Indie rock showcase featuring Small Yards, The Hugs, and Media House at Seattle's oldest bar in Pioneer Square.

items.title: Don't Worry, Margaret's Sick, Pank Pariah
items.description: Indie and alternative rock bands perform at this Capitol Hill mainstay for local music.
